questions.question: What is the most important field in a tool definition, and why?
options: `description` — Claude uses it to decide whether to call the tool and which tool to call
`name` — must be unique and clearly identifies the tool to Claude
`input_schema` — defines the exact data structure Claude must provide
`required` — determines when Claude is obligated to call the tool
questions.explanation: A vague description causes missed or wrong tool calls. The description should explain what the tool does, when to use it, and importantly, when NOT to use it.

questions.question: What `stop_reason` value tells you that Claude wants to call a tool?
options: "tool_use" — Claude has produced one or more tool_use content blocks
"function_call" — the term used when tool execution is requested
"stop" — the generic signal for any non-natural termination
"end_turn" — Claude has finished its reasoning and is ready to act
questions.explanation: stop_reason: "tool_use" means Claude produced tool_use content blocks. You must execute each one and return tool_result blocks before generation can continue.

questions.question: A `tool_use` content block contains an `id` field. When and how is this ID used?
options: As `tool_use_id` in the matching `tool_result` block — it maps results back to the correct call
For logging and debugging tool call sequences in your application
As the session cache key for caching repeated tool results
To deduplicate parallel tool calls that have identical arguments
questions.explanation: Each tool_result must include a tool_use_id that exactly matches the id of the corresponding tool_use block. Without this match, Claude cannot map the result to the right call.

questions.question: Claude calls a tool but your execution code returns a 503 error. How should you handle this?
options: Return a `tool_result` with `is_error: true` and an error message as the content
Silently retry the tool execution up to three times before notifying Claude
Omit the tool_result — Claude will detect the missing result and retry automatically
Return an empty tool_result with a null content field
questions.explanation: Never silently ignore a tool call. Return is_error: true with an error message so Claude can acknowledge the failure, explain it to the user, and potentially suggest alternatives.

questions.question: What `tool_choice` value forces Claude to call one specific named tool?
options: {"type": "tool", "name": "<tool_name>"} — forces that exact tool to be called
{"type": "required"} — forces Claude to call any available tool
{"type": "forced", "tool": "<tool_name>"} — the forced-tool syntax
Listing that tool as the only entry in the tools array
questions.explanation: {"type": "tool", "name": "..."} forces a specific tool. {"type": "required"} forces any tool. {"type": "auto"} lets Claude decide. {"type": "none"} prevents all tool calls.

questions.question: Claude produces two `tool_use` blocks in a single response. What does this mean and how do you handle it?
options: Parallel tool calls — execute both independently and return both tool_results in one user message
An error state — Claude should never produce more than one tool call per response
Sequential calls — execute the first, return its result, then Claude will request the second
Ambiguity — Claude is uncertain which tool to use; only execute the first and await clarification
questions.explanation: Multiple tool_use blocks in one response mean parallel calls — Claude believes they are independent. Return all tool_results in the same user message turn. You can execute them concurrently.

questions.question: You want to extract structured data from documents and always receive a typed JSON object without string parsing. What is the tool-based approach?
options: Define a tool whose `input_schema` matches the target structure and set `tool_choice` to force it
Set `response_format: {"type": "json_object"}` and parse the text response
Use assistant prefilling with "{" and extract the JSON from the text output
Define the JSON schema in the system prompt and instruct Claude to follow it strictly
questions.explanation: When Claude is forced to call a tool, the tool_use block's input field is already a parsed dict matching your input_schema — no JSON string parsing needed.

questions.question: What JSON Schema type declaration allows a field to be either a number or null?
options: "type": ["number", "null"]
"type": "number", "nullable": true
"type": "number", "optional": true
"type": "number", "default": null
questions.explanation: An array of types — ["number", "null"] — is the correct JSON Schema syntax for a field that accepts a number or null. This signals the field is optional and Claude should use null when the value is absent.

questions.question: After you send a `tool_result`, Claude's next response has `stop_reason: "tool_use"` again. What happened and what should you do?
options: Claude is chaining tools — continue the loop, executing tool calls and returning results until `stop_reason` is `"end_turn"`
An error in your tool_result format caused Claude to retry the same call
Claude is parallelizing — return this new result simultaneously with the previous one
The context window is almost full — start a new conversation session
questions.explanation: Tool chaining is normal in agentic workflows. Claude needs the first tool's result to decide what to call next. Keep looping until stop_reason is end_turn.

questions.question: What does `tool_choice: {"type": "none"}` do?
options: Prevents Claude from calling any tools for that request, even if tools are defined
Allows Claude to choose whether to call a tool or generate text
Removes all tools from the request temporarily without modifying the tools array
Marks all tools as optional hints for that turn only
questions.explanation: "none" disables tool calling entirely for that request. Useful when you need a text response mid-workflow without allowing Claude to make further tool calls.

questions.question: A tool's `input_schema` has a `status` property but no `required` field. What does Claude do if the status is not mentioned by the user?
options: Claude may omit the field entirely — without `required`, all properties are optional
Claude asks the user to supply the missing value before completing the tool call
Claude infers a default value based on the field name and description
The API returns a 400 error because the schema is incomplete
questions.explanation: Without a `required` array, all properties are optional in JSON Schema. Claude may omit them. Add `"required": ["status"]` if the field is mandatory.

questions.question: Why must tool names match the pattern `^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,64}$`?
options: The API requires it — spaces and special characters cause a 400 invalid_request_error
Snake_case is a style convention; any format works at the API level
The pattern improves Claude's ability to reason about tool names semantically
Tool names are compiled into function signatures that require valid identifier syntax
questions.explanation: This is an API constraint, not just style. Violating it returns a 400 error. Keep names to alphanumeric characters, underscores, and hyphens, max 64 characters.

questions.question: You have 10 tools available and Claude consistently calls the wrong one. What is the most likely cause?
options: Poor or ambiguous tool descriptions — with many tools, descriptions are the primary selection signal
Too many tools — the API recommends no more than 5 tools per request
The correct tool is listed too late in the tools array
`tool_choice` should be set to `"required"` to force Claude to pick a tool
questions.explanation: With many tools, Claude relies almost entirely on descriptions to distinguish between them. Make each tool's scope, trigger conditions, and exclusions explicit and unambiguous.

questions.question: What happens if you forget to append the assistant's `tool_use` response to the messages array before sending the `tool_result`?
options: The conversation becomes invalid — Claude sees a tool_result with no matching tool_use, causing a 400 error
The tool_result is silently ignored and Claude generates a text response instead
Claude infers the missing tool_use from context and processes the result anyway
A 422 unprocessable entity error is returned indicating unexpected message ordering
questions.explanation: The full assistant message (including all tool_use blocks) must always be appended before appending the tool_result message. Skipping this creates an invalid conversation state.

questions.question: True or false: Claude executes tool code on Anthropic's servers when a tool call is made.
options: False — Claude only produces a `tool_use` request; your code is always responsible for execution
True — Claude executes sandboxed tool code server-side and returns the output
True for built-in Anthropic tools; false for custom tools defined by the developer
True only for computer-use tools; all other tool types are executed client-side
questions.explanation: Claude never runs code. It produces tool_use content blocks as structured requests. Your application receives those requests, executes the actual logic, and returns results via tool_result blocks.
